'''Sébastien Raguin''' (born 14 February 1980)<ref name=Dragons/> is a French former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Catalans Dragons]] in the [[Super League]]; he played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|ce}}. He is the current first team coach for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL League 1]].

==Background==
Raguin was born in [[Toulouse]], [[France]].<ref name=Dragons/>

==Career==
Raguin first caught the eye in 2005 playing for [[Toulouse Olympique]] during their famous run to the semi-finals of the [[Challenge Cup]]. He famously played for them semi-professionally whilst working during the week as a traffic warden in the city of [[Toulouse]].

In 2007 he signed with the Catalans Dragons, and quickly established himself as a key component of the Catalans pack. His 2007 season was punctuated by injury, however he became an ever-present in the Catalans Dragons side during their run to the Super League play-offs in 2008, where he frequently played in the centres striking up a good partnership with Younes Khattabi.

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#France|France training squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="France dominated by Dragons">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12196_3949078,00.html|title=France dominated by Dragons|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2008-08-07|access-date=2008-08-08}}</ref>

He was named in the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup squads#France|France squad]] for the [[2008 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="France name official squad">{{cite web|url=http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx|title=France name official squad|publisher=RLWC2008|date=2008-09-24|access-date=2008-10-03|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080930072635/http://www.rlwc08.com/news/news/archive/2008/09/24/france-name-official-squad.aspx|archive-date=2008-09-30}}</ref>
